expectations.text: Output contains all four sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Output has '## Mission', '## Goals', '## Premises', and '## Constraints' as distinct H2 headings.

expectations.text: Mission section contains a single sentence or short statement (not a list), expressing the core invariant purpose
expectations.evidence: Mission section contains a multi-sentence paragraph followed by a blockquote with a 'Core invariant' restatement. The main statement is: 'Provide an open-source, self-hostable project management platform that lets teams track work items, run iterative cycles, manage product roadmaps, and collaborate on documents -- without the overhead of managing the tool itself.' While this is one sentence, it is followed by a second statement in a blockquote. More importantly, the mission reads as a feature list ('track work items, run iterative cycles, manage product roadmaps, and collaborate on documents') rather than an invariant purpose. However, it is technically a single sentence at its core. PASS on form, but the mission is more feature-descriptive than purpose-invariant.

expectations.text: Goals section contains specific, measurable outcomes (e.g. feature capabilities, user actions) rather than vague aspirations
expectations.evidence: Goals include specific outcomes: 'Replace proprietary PM tools (Jira, Linear, Monday, ClickUp)', 'real-time collaboration on documents and boards', 'i18n package with IntlMessageFormat', 'AI-assisted workflows (Pages with AI capabilities)'. The Measurability column adds specificity: 'Both paths documented and tested' (G-3), 'Live service operational; concurrent editing without conflicts' (G-4).

expectations.text: Constraints section captures actual hard boundaries (e.g. deployment model, license, tech stack, integrations) distinct from goals
expectations.evidence: Constraints include: 'AGPL-3.0 license' (C-1), 'PostgreSQL 15 as the sole relational database' (C-4), 'React + React Router frontend with Vite build tooling' (C-8), 'pnpm as the exclusive package manager' (C-11), 'Minimum 12 GB RAM' (C-14). These are hard boundaries distinct from goals.

expectations.text: Output references specific content from the Plane GitHub repo (e.g. specific features, project structure, technology choices) — not just generic PM tool boilerplate
expectations.evidence: References repo-specific content: 'apps/api/plane/' directory, 'apps/live' service, 'apps/space', 'packages/shared-state', 'God Mode for instance administration', 'Valkey/Redis 7.2', 'RabbitMQ 3.13', 'PostgreSQL 15', domain model list 'workspace, project, issue, issue_type, cycle, module, page...' from apps/api/plane/db/models/. Appendix lists 15 shared packages by name.

expectations.text: At least one Premise or Constraint is implicit (not stated word-for-word in the source) — e.g. assumptions about users, deployment environment, or integration ecosystem
expectations.evidence: P-4 'Project management tools should not require dedicated administrators' is inferred from the tagline 'Without the chaos of managing the tool itself'. P-6 'Real-time collaboration is expected in modern PM tools' is inferred from the existence of the live service. P-9 'Background job processing is required for scalability' is inferred from architecture. These are implicit, not stated word-for-word.

expectations.text: Output shows evidence of bottom-up Chain of Knowledge expansion with multiple levels visible (e.g. L5 Topics through L1 Domains, or equivalent layered knowledge build-up from raw facts to domain-level synthesis)
expectations.evidence: No CoK expansion, no L5-L1 levels, no saturation summary. The output goes directly from source listing to Mission/Goals/Premises/Constraints without any visible bottom-up knowledge build-up methodology.

expectations.text: Every constraint (both hard and soft) cites its source — who or what imposed it (e.g. 'MIT license file', 'README', 'architecture doc', 'inferred from tech stack') — not just the constraint text alone
expectations.evidence: Constraints are organized by category (Licensing, Technical Architecture, Code Quality, Data and Storage, Internationalization) but none have source citations. The tables have only 'ID' and 'Constraint' columns -- no 'Source' column. For example, C-1 says 'AGPL-3.0 license' but does not cite LICENSE.txt or any source. C-14 says 'Minimum 12 GB RAM' but does not cite CONTRIBUTING.md.

claims.claim: The output identifies 26 constraints across 5 categories
claims.evidence: Counted C-1 through C-26 across Licensing/Legal, Technical Architecture, Code Quality/Process, Data/Storage, and Internationalization sections

claims.claim: 15+ shared packages exist in the monorepo
claims.evidence: Appendix lists 15 named packages: types, ui, editor, shared-state, hooks, i18n, constants, utils, services, logger, propel, decorators, codemods, tailwind-config, typescript-config

eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill output is thorough on content but lacks the methodological structure (CoK expansion, source citations on constraints) that the assertions test. This is a genuine skill vs. no-skill differentiation.
